overall_assessment.fls_score_context: 433 FLS score represents solid competent performance - well above passing threshold with excellent knot technique and minimal penalties.
overall_assessment.session_headline: Strong technical execution with perfect knot sequence - focus next on speed optimization and placement precision.

rubric_insights.observation: 147s completion time is reasonable but leaves room for improvement - needle loading took 18s which could be streamlined
rubric_insights.impact_on_score: Time component cost approximately 50-60 points compared to sub-120s performance

rubric_insights.observation: Perfect knot sequence with proper surgeon's knot and correct hand switching - zero penalties here
rubric_insights.impact_on_score: Full points earned - this is a significant strength

rubric_insights.observation: 3mm total deviation from target marks resulted in 3-point penalty
rubric_insights.impact_on_score: Lost 3 points due to placement accuracy - relatively minor but improvable

rubric_insights.observation: Excellent closure with no visible gaps - perfect execution
rubric_insights.impact_on_score: Full points earned for closure quality

technique_coaching.observation: In frames 2-3, needle loading appears methodical but could be more efficient - taking 18s for this phase
technique_coaching.why_it_matters: Faster needle loading directly improves overall time without compromising safety or accuracy
technique_coaching.correction: Practice loading needle at 2/3 point from tip in one smooth motion rather than multiple adjustments
technique_coaching.drill: Load and unload needle 25 times focusing on single-motion pickup at correct grasp point

technique_coaching.observation: Frame 4 shows steady positioning but 1.5mm deviation at each mark suggests slight targeting inconsistency
technique_coaching.why_it_matters: Precise placement is critical for real surgical applications and FLS scoring
technique_coaching.correction: Take extra moment to align needle perpendicular to tissue before driving through marks
technique_coaching.drill: Practice 20 needle passes through marked targets focusing on perpendicular entry angle

technique_coaching.observation: Frames 5-11 show excellent knot technique with proper surgeon's knot and hand switching - this is exemplary
technique_coaching.why_it_matters: This level of knot technique is exactly what's needed for surgical competency
technique_coaching.correction: No correction needed - maintain this exact technique
technique_coaching.drill: Continue current knot practice to maintain muscle memory

strengths_to_reinforce.observation: Perfect surgeon's knot formation in frames 5-7 with proper double wrap and secure tightening
strengths_to_reinforce.why_it_matters: Surgeon's knot is the foundation of secure surgical knots - your technique here is textbook
strengths_to_reinforce.encouragement: Your knot technique is at expert level - this gives you a huge advantage as you work on speed

strengths_to_reinforce.observation: Excellent hand switching sequence shown in frames 8 and 10 - proper alternating technique throughout
strengths_to_reinforce.why_it_matters: Correct hand switching prevents knot slippage and is essential for surgical safety
strengths_to_reinforce.encouragement: Your hand switching is automatic and correct - this advanced skill will serve you well in real surgery

strengths_to_reinforce.observation: Frame 22 shows perfect drain closure with no gaps - excellent final result
strengths_to_reinforce.why_it_matters: Complete closure prevents fluid leakage and demonstrates proper tension management
strengths_to_reinforce.encouragement: Your closure technique achieves the exact clinical outcome needed - focus on maintaining this quality while improving speed

practice_plan.session_focus: Streamline needle loading speed while maintaining current knot excellence
practice_plan.warmup_drill: 5 minutes of needle loading practice - focus on single-motion pickup at 2/3 point

primary_drill.name: Speed Loading Challenge
primary_drill.description: Load needle correctly, place single suture through two marks, tie surgeon's knot + 2 throws, cut. Focus on reducing needle loading time to under 10 seconds while maintaining placement accuracy.
primary_drill.success_criteria: Needle loaded in <10s, placement within 2mm of marks, knot sequence maintained

practice_plan.cooldown: Review your fastest rep - what felt different about the needle loading motion?
practice_plan.estimated_sessions_to_improvement: 3-4 focused sessions should bring you to sub-120s consistently

progress_context.vs_baseline: Significant improvement from early 600s scores to consistent 400s-500s range
progress_context.vs_peak: This 433 is near your best performance - you're approaching your current ceiling
progress_context.milestone_note: Next target: Sub-120s completion time while maintaining this knot quality (would push score to 500+)

coaching_limitations: Camera angle makes it difficult to assess exact needle grasp point during loading phase - recommendations based on timing rather than direct visualization of grip position
